newsSinceJun6: No material new news since June 6, 2026. The major catalysts - Q3 FY26 beat (EPS $0.27 vs ~$0.06 est., revenue $236M +11.5% organic, record $348M bookings, 1.48 book-to-bill, record ~$1.6B backlog) and the late-May Blue Raven order for 1,000 RTBX06 BuiltSECURE servers plus Altera/VadaTech Agilex 9 FPGA alliances - all predate June 6 and drove the ~22% post-earnings run. Today's -4.43% has no identifiable fundamental driver and reads as flow-driven consolidation after the rerating; Mercury is exhibiting at IMS 2026 in Boston June 7-12 (non-material).

sources.label: Yahoo Finance - Mercury Systems (MRCY) Up 21.7% Since Last Earnings Report
Simply Wall St - MRCY Up 13.6% After Record Blue Raven Server Deal And New FPGA Alliances
Mercury Systems Investor Relations - Press Releases

verdicts.rankRationale: No new information since yesterday's hold; the earnings/Blue Raven catalysts are pre-June 6 and already priced into the +39% YTD rerating. Today's -4.4% pullback on no news modestly improves entry asymmetry but is not sharp enough to change the latent-node setup, so hold with slight positive drift.
verdicts.bull: Record $1.6B backlog, 1.48 book-to-bill, and the largest-ever RTBX06 order confirm the operating turnaround is real, and a -4.4% no-news pullback gives a slightly better entry into a validated defense-edge-compute thesis.
verdicts.neutral: The 'crowd-skeptical turnaround' thesis is being validated, but validation is exactly what erodes latency - the stock is +39% YTD and +22% post-earnings, so residual hidden alpha is shrinking even as fundamentals improve.
verdicts.bear: After the rerating, MRCY is no longer under-owned at $6.5B cap; any FY27 guidance softness, margin slippage, or defense budget timing noise could unwind the multiple expansion faster than backlog converts to revenue.
verdicts.primaryCatalyst: Q4 FY26 earnings and FY27 guidance confirming sustained margin recovery and backlog-to-revenue conversion; follow-on common-processing-architecture orders.
verdicts.primaryRisk: Post-rerating crowding: at +39% YTD the turnaround is increasingly consensus, so execution wobbles or program delays would hit a much fuller multiple.
